# `sesam status` renders a tree of secrets with a per-secret state glyph.
# In-sync secrets are hidden by default and shown with --all; --json stays the
# stable, unfiltered machine interface.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Init creates README.md, registers it as a secret, and seals it.
exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# --json is unfiltered: the in-sync README still appears.
exec sesam status --json
stdout 'README.md'
stdout '"in_sync"'

# Default tree hides in-sync secrets; README is not listed, but the footer
# still counts it.
exec sesam status
! stdout 'README\.md'
stdout 'in sync'

# --all reveals the in-sync secret in the tree.
exec sesam status --all
stdout 'README\.md'

# Modify the revealed secret: it is now drift and shows by default.
cp modified_readme.md README.md
exec sesam status
stdout 'README\.md'
stdout 'out of sync'

exec sesam status --json
stdout '"out_of_sync"'

# --diff shells out to git to show old (sealed) vs new (revealed) content.
exec sesam status --diff
stdout 'modified readme'

# Removing the revealed plaintext reports it as unrevealed.
rm README.md
exec sesam status --json
stdout '"unrevealed"'

-- modified_readme.md --
# modified readme

This is the new content after the change.
